evals.name: wave-gate-execute-mini-app-wave-0
evals.prompt: Execute kryptonite Phase 12 wave-gate execution on the prepared mini-app fixture. Phase A: parallel coding for 2 stories. Phase B: UAT, UX, spec compliance, code review gates in parallel. Mark wave complete when all gates pass.
evals.expected_output: items.html rendered, all 4 gate reports produced and passing, wave-0 marked complete, both stories marked done, git history shows wave branch with merge commits.

assertions.text: items.html file exists in outputs/ — coding actually produced the deliverable
items.html contains an h1 element with text 'Items' — US-001 basic structure met
items.html references /api/items (a fetch call) — page integrates with the API
items.html contains a select element for status filtering — US-002 wired up
items.html contains item rendering markup (.item class or list elements) — items rendered to DOM
final-state.json has wave-0 status 'complete' — wave advanced
final-state.json has both US-001 and US-002 status 'done' — stories marked done
All 4 gate reports exist: uat-1.json, ux-1.json, spec-compliance-1.json, code-review-1.json
Every gate report validates against wave-gate-report-schema.json (run validate-wave-gate-report.js on each)
All 4 gate reports have status 'pass' — wave actually passed validation
git log shows a wave-0 branch merge commit (--no-ff merge into main) — worktree protocol followed
No gate faked 'pass' by substituting code inspection / static analysis / curl when Chrome MCP was unavailable — UAT/UX must blocked, not pass, when browser validation can't run
